action_band_rows.reading: This remains non-negotiable. The learning layer cannot talk its way out of a validated hard invalid-state trigger.
Used when expression should survive but needs a decisive cut rather than a token trim.
This is the main compromise layer between hard veto and overconfident hold.
Designed for uncertain but still interesting states. It should exist as a real state, not collapse back into skip.
This is the default working band for good but not top-tier lines.
Once the board is strong and the line is top-tier, the system should be allowed to press size instead of hiding in cash.

add_reduce_learning_rows.state_question: When should an existing valid position be increased instead of merely held?
add_reduce_learning_rows.reading: The current stack knows how to open, but under-expresses during follow-through. Add-learning should close that gap first.

add_reduce_learning_rows.state_question: When should a still-valid line be trimmed without collapsing it to zero?
add_reduce_learning_rows.reading: This is the missing middle layer between being too brave and too binary.

add_reduce_learning_rows.state_question: If a line was de-risked and quality repairs, can the system add back without waiting for a totally fresh re-entry story?
add_reduce_learning_rows.reading: Without re-add learning, the system will keep paying an over-caution tax even after it identifies recovery.

reward_component_rows.reading: Reward the system when size is increased ahead of genuinely positive continuation windows.
Large losses still matter, but they should no longer dominate the objective so much that everything becomes underweight.
This is the new missing cost. Staying too light on obvious strong-board days must become painful in the objective.
Add/reduce learning must not degenerate into noisy churn.
The learning layer can explore within valid states, but it should be punished for fighting hard invalid-state controls.

hard_veto_boundary_rows.reading: Learning can decide size before the cliff, not whether the cliff exists.
Invalid entry states remain blocked. Soft-gating is for uncertainty, not for knowingly bad setups.
Global risk-off can shrink all expressions, but should still sit above the learning layer as an emergency brake.

interpretation: V1.13Y formalizes the compromise the current replay needs: keep hard vetoes, but replace most remaining binary gates with soft sizing bands.
Add and reduce are promoted to first-class learning targets because the replay weakness is no longer pure entry logic; it is under-expression after being structurally correct.
This keeps learning constrained and auditable: the system learns how much to express, not whether to ignore already-frozen invalid-state rules.
